**[VOTING AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

Dated as of November 3, 2021

This VOTING AGREEMENT (this \"Agreement\") is entered into as of November 3, 2021, by and among:

**Terravox Systems, Inc.**, a Delaware corporation (the \"Company\");

**Marcus Ellery** (\"Ellery\");

**Priya Chandrasekaran** (\"Chandrasekaran,\" and together with Ellery, the \"Founders\");

**Ridgeline Ventures Fund II, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership (\"Ridgeline\");

**Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership (\"Northbridge\"); and

**Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership (\"Stonemill,\" and together with Ridgeline and Northbridge, the \"Investor Stockholders,\" and each an \"Investor Stockholder\").

The Company, the Founders, and the Investor Stockholders are referred to herein collectively as the \"Parties\" and each individually as a \"Party.\"

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

WHEREAS, the Company is a Delaware corporation engaged in the development and sale of industrial IoT sensor platforms and predictive analytics software;

WHEREAS, the Company has authorized capital stock consisting of 50,000,000 shares of Common Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, 5,000,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, 7,000,000 shares of Series B Preferred Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, and 10,000,000 shares of Series C Preferred Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, as set forth in the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation of the Company, dated as of November 3, 2021 (the \"Restated Certificate\");

WHEREAS, the Founders and the Investor Stockholders hold shares of the Company\'s capital stock as set forth on Exhibit A attached hereto;

WHEREAS, the Company and the parties hereto are simultaneously entering into that certain Stockholders\' Agreement, dated as of the date hereof (the \"Stockholders\' Agreement\"), and that certain Registration Rights Agreement, dated as of the date hereof (the \"Registration Rights Agreement\");

WHEREAS, the parties desire to enter into this Agreement to set forth certain agreements regarding the voting of their shares and the composition of the Board of Directors of the Company (the \"Board\");

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements herein contained, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties agree as follows:

**[1. Definitions]{.underline}**

As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings ascribed to them below:

**\"Board\"** means the Board of Directors of the Company.

**\"Common Stock\"** means the Common Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, of the Company.

**\"Preferred Stock\"** means, collectively, the Series A Preferred Stock, Series B Preferred Stock, and Series C Preferred Stock of the Company.

**\"Restated Certificate\"** means the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation of the Company, filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on November 3, 2021, as the same may be amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time.

**\"Sale of the Company\"** means (a) any merger, consolidation, or reorganization of the Company in which the stockholders of the Company immediately prior to such transaction do not retain a majority of the voting power of the surviving or resulting entity, (b) a sale, lease, exclusive license, or other disposition of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company, or (c) any transaction or series of related transactions resulting in the transfer of more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding voting power of the Company.

**\"Shares\"** means, with respect to any Party, all shares of Common Stock and Preferred Stock (on an as-converted to Common Stock basis) now or hereafter owned or held by such Party, including any shares of Common Stock or Preferred Stock acquired after the date hereof by purchase, issuance, conversion, exercise of options or warrants, or otherwise.

**\"Transfer\"** means any direct or indirect sale, assignment, transfer, pledge, hypothecation, encumbrance, or other disposition of Shares or any interest therein, whether voluntary or involuntary, including by operation of law.

**[2. Board Composition]{.underline}**

**2.1 Board Size**

The number of directors constituting the Board shall be seven (7). No Party shall take any action to increase or decrease the size of the Board without the prior written consent of a majority of the Investor Stockholders.

**2.2 Designation Rights**

The Board shall be composed as follows:

> **(a)** One (1) director designated by Ridgeline Ventures Fund II, LP, for so long as Ridgeline holds at least 1,000,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock (or Common Stock issued upon conversion thereof) (the \"Series A Director,\" initially David Kleinhans);
>
> **(b)** One (1) director designated by Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP, for so long as Northbridge holds at least 1,000,000 shares of Series B Preferred Stock (or Common Stock issued upon conversion thereof) (the \"Series B Director,\" initially Sandra Okafor);
>
> **(c)** One (1) director designated by Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP, for so long as Stonemill holds at least 1,000,000 shares of Series C Preferred Stock (or Common Stock issued upon conversion thereof) (the \"Series C Director,\" initially Julian Trask);
>
> **(d)** The Chief Executive Officer of the Company (the \"CEO Director,\" initially Marcus Ellery);
>
> **(e)** One (1) director designated by the Founders, other than the CEO Director (the \"Founder Director,\" initially Priya Chandrasekaran);
>
> **(f)** Two (2) independent directors mutually agreed upon by the CEO Director and a majority of the Investor Stockholders (the \"Independent Directors,\" initially Rebecca Lau and Thomas Garrett). Independent directors must not be employees of the Company or affiliates of any Investor Stockholder.

**2.3 Removal and Vacancies**

A director designated pursuant to Section 2.2 may be removed from the Board (with or without cause) only upon the written request of the Party entitled to designate such director. Any vacancy created by such removal or by the resignation, death, or incapacity of a designated director shall be filled by a new designee of the Party entitled to make such designation. If an Investor Stockholder ceases to hold the minimum number of shares specified in Section 2.2 for its designation right, that Investor Stockholder\'s designation right shall terminate, and the vacancy shall be filled by the Board.

**2.4 Committee Composition**

Committees of the Board shall be constituted as determined by the Board; *provided, however*, that unless otherwise agreed in writing by the Investor Stockholders, each committee of the Board shall include at least one (1) Investor Stockholder designee.

**[3. Voting Obligations]{.underline}**

**3.1 Agreement to Vote**

Each Party agrees to vote (or cause to be voted) all Shares held by such Party, and to take all other necessary or desirable actions within such Party\'s control (whether at any annual or special meeting of stockholders, by written consent in lieu of a meeting, or otherwise), to ensure that the composition of the Board is as set forth in Section 2.2, including without limitation:

> **(a)** voting all Shares in favor of each director designated in accordance with Section 2.2;
>
> **(b)** voting all Shares against the removal of any director designated in accordance with Section 2.2, except upon the written request of the Party entitled to designate such director; and
>
> **(c)** voting all Shares against any increase or decrease in the authorized size of the Board unless approved by a majority of the Investor Stockholders.

**3.2 No Other Voting Obligations**

Except as expressly set forth in this Section 3, this Agreement shall not be construed to create any obligation on the part of any Party to vote Shares in any particular manner with respect to any matter submitted to a vote of the stockholders of the Company, including without limitation any proposed merger, consolidation, sale of assets, or other corporate transaction.

**3.3 Specific Performance**

Each Party acknowledges that the obligations under this Section 3 are unique and that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the non-breaching Parties for any breach of the provisions of this Section 3. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the other Parties shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Section 3, without the necessity of proving the inadequacy of monetary damages or posting any bond or other security.

**3.4 Written Consent**

For the avoidance of doubt, the voting obligations set forth in this Section 3 shall apply to actions taken by written consent in lieu of a meeting to the same extent as actions taken at any annual or special meeting of stockholders.

**[4. Board Observer]{.underline}**

**4.1 Observer Right**

For so long as Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP holds at least 1,000,000 shares of Series C Preferred Stock (or Common Stock issued upon conversion thereof), Stonemill shall be entitled to appoint one (1) non-voting observer (the \"Observer\") to attend all meetings of the Board (including meetings of any committee thereof). The Observer shall be entitled to receive copies of all materials and information provided to the Board members in connection with such meetings at the same time as such materials are provided to Board members. The initial Observer shall be designated by Stonemill in writing to the Company.

**4.2 Limitations.** The Observer shall not be entitled to vote on any matter before the Board or any committee thereof. The Company shall have the right to exclude the Observer from any portion of a Board or committee meeting, and to withhold any materials, if and to the extent that the Board determines in good faith, upon advice of counsel, that such exclusion or withholding is necessary to preserve attorney-client privilege or to address a bona fide conflict of interest between Stonemill and the Company.

**4.3 Confidentiality.** The Observer shall be required to execute and deliver to the Company a confidentiality and non-disclosure agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Company prior to attending any Board meeting or receiving any Board materials.

**[5. Representations and Warranties]{.underline}**

**5.1 Representations and Warranties of Each Party**

Each Party represents and warrants to each other Party as follows:

> **(a)** Such Party has full power and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement;
>
> **(b)** This Agreement constitutes the valid and binding obligation of such Party, enforceable against such Party in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, and similar laws affecting creditors\' rights generally and to general principles of equity;
>
> **(c)** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by such Party does not and will not conflict with, violate, or result in a breach of any material agreement, instrument, order, judgment, or decree to which such Party is a party or by which such Party is bound; and
>
> **(d)** The Shares listed on Exhibit A opposite such Party\'s name are the only shares of the Company\'s capital stock held by such Party as of the date hereof.

**[6. Additional Shares]{.underline}**

**6.1 Additional Shares Subject to Agreement**

If any Party acquires additional shares of Common Stock or Preferred Stock of the Company after the date hereof (whether by purchase, issuance, conversion, exercise of options or warrants, or otherwise), such additional shares shall automatically become subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and shall be deemed \"Shares\" hereunder.

**6.2 Transfers Subject to Agreement**

No Party shall Transfer any Shares to any person or entity unless such transferee agrees in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as if such transferee were an original party hereto. Any Transfer in violation of this Section 6.2 shall be void and of no effect.

**[7. Term and Termination]{.underline}**

**7.1 Term.** This Agreement shall become effective as of the date first written above and shall continue in full force and effect until the earliest to occur of:

> **(a)** the closing of a firm commitment underwritten initial public offering of the Company\'s Common Stock pursuant to an effective registration statement filed under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, with aggregate gross proceeds to the Company of not less than \$75,000,000 (an \"IPO\");
>
> **(b)** the consummation of a Sale of the Company (as defined in Section 1);
>
> **(c)** the written consent of (i) the Company, (ii) the holders of a majority of the then-outstanding shares of Common Stock held by parties to this Agreement, and (iii) the holders of a majority of each series of Preferred Stock held by parties to this Agreement, each voting as a separate class; or
>
> **(d)** November 3, 2026 (the fifth (5th) anniversary of the date of this Agreement).

**7.2 Effect of Termination**

Upon the termination of this Agreement pursuant to Section 7.1, all rights and obligations of the Parties hereunder shall terminate and be of no further force or effect; *provided, however*, that (i) no termination shall relieve any Party of liability for any breach of this Agreement prior to such termination, and (ii) the provisions of Section 8 (Miscellaneous) shall survive any termination of this Agreement.

**7.3 Expiration of Individual Rights**

Notwithstanding Section 7.1, the designation right of any Investor Stockholder under Section 2.2 shall terminate with respect to such Investor Stockholder if such Investor Stockholder ceases to hold the minimum number of Shares specified therein for the applicable designation right. The Observer right under Section 4 shall terminate if Stonemill ceases to hold at least 1,000,000 shares of Series C Preferred Stock (or Common Stock issued upon conversion thereof).

**[8. Miscellaneous]{.underline}**

**8.1 Governing Law**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to principles of conflicts of law that would require the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

**8.2 Jurisdiction.** Each Party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware (or, if the Court of Chancery declines to accept jurisdiction, any state or federal court within the State of Delaware) for any action, suit, or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and each Party irrevocably waives any objection to venue in such courts, including any claim of inconvenient forum.

**8.3 Entire Agreement**

This Agreement, together with the Stockholders\' Agreement, the Registration Rights Agreement, and the Restated Certificate, constitutes the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, whether written or oral, with respect to such subject matter. In the event of any conflict between the terms of this Agreement and the Restated Certificate, the terms of the Restated Certificate shall control.

**8.4 Amendments and Waivers**

This Agreement may be amended, or any provision hereof may be waived, only by a written instrument signed by (a) the Company, (b) the holders of a majority of the shares of Common Stock then held by the Founders, and (c) the holders of a majority of the shares of Preferred Stock (on an as-converted basis) then held by the Investor Stockholders, voting together as a single class. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any amendment or waiver that would adversely and disproportionately affect the rights of any Party under this Agreement shall require the prior written consent of such affected Party.

**8.5 Notices.** All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed effectively given upon (a) personal delivery to the Party to be notified, (b) one (1) business day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier service, prepaid, or (c) three (3) business days after deposit in the United States mail, by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, addressed to the Company at 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, Attention: Chief Executive Officer, and to each other Party at the address set forth on Exhibit A hereto, or at such other address as a Party may designate by written notice to the other Parties in accordance with this Section 8.5.

**8.6 Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

**8.7 Successors and Assigns**

This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto and their respective successors, assigns, heirs, executors, administrators, and legal representatives. No Party may assign this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Parties, except in connection with a Permitted Transfer (as defined in the Stockholders\' Agreement).

**8.8 Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same agreement. Execution and delivery of this Agreement by facsimile or electronic transmission (including in .pdf format) shall be deemed to be an original execution and delivery for all purposes.

**8.9 No Third-Party Beneficiaries**

This Agreement is for the sole benefit of the Parties hereto and their respective permitted successors and assigns, and nothing herein, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any other person or entity any right, benefit, or remedy of any nature whatsoever under or by reason of this Agreement.

**8.10 Further Assurances**

Each Party agrees to execute and deliver such further instruments and documents and to take such other action as may be reasonably necessary or desirable to carry out the purposes and intent of this Agreement.

**8.11 Legend.** Each certificate or book-entry statement representing Shares subject to this Agreement shall bear a legend substantially as follows:

> \"THE SHARES REPRESENTED HEREBY ARE SUBJECT TO THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF A VOTING AGREEMENT DATED AS OF NOVEMBER 3, 2021, A COPY OF WHICH IS ON FILE WITH THE SECRETARY OF THE COMPANY.\"

*\[Remainder of Page Intentionally Left Blank --- Signature Pages Follow\]*

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Voting Agreement as of the date first written above.

**[REGISTRATION RIGHTS AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

This Registration Rights Agreement (this **\"Agreement\"**) is entered into as of November 3, 2021, by and among Terravox Systems, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the **\"Company\"**), and each of the investors listed on Schedule A attached hereto (each, an **\"Investor\"** and collectively, the **\"Investors\"**).

**WHEREAS**, the Company and the Investors are parties to that certain Series C Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement, dated as of November 3, 2021 (the **\"Purchase Agreement\"**);

**WHEREAS**, in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Purchase Agreement, the Company has agreed to provide the registration rights set forth herein;

**WHEREAS**, the Company previously entered into that certain Registration Rights Agreement, dated as of August 9, 2017, as amended February 22, 2019 (the **\"Prior Agreement\"**), and the parties hereto desire to amend and restate the Prior Agreement in its entirety;

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties agree as follows:

**[Section 1. Definitions]{.underline}**

As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

**\"Commission\"** means the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission or any successor agency administering the Securities Act or the Exchange Act.

**\"Common Stock\"** means the Common Stock of the Company, par value \$0.001 per share.

**\"Company\"** means Terravox Systems, Inc., a Delaware corporation.

**\"Deferral Period\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 2.1(d).

**\"Demand Registration\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 2.1(a).

**\"Exchange Act\"** means the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder.

**\"Form S-1\"** means a registration statement on Form S-1 under the Securities Act or any successor form thereto.

**\"Form S-3\"** means a registration statement on Form S-3 under the Securities Act or any successor form thereto.

**\"Holder\"** or **\"Holders\"** means each Investor and any permitted transferee or assignee of Registrable Securities who has become a party to this Agreement in accordance with Section 10.

**\"Initiating Holders\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 2.1(a).

**\"IPO\"** means the Company\'s first underwritten public offering of Common Stock under the Securities Act.

**\"Preferred Stock\"** means, collectively, the Series A Preferred Stock, Series B Preferred Stock, and Series C Preferred Stock of the Company.

**\"Registrable Securities\"** means (i) shares of Common Stock issued or issuable upon conversion of the Preferred Stock held by the Holders, and (ii) any shares of Common Stock issued as a dividend or other distribution with respect to, or in exchange for or in replacement of, the shares referenced in clause (i). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Registrable Securities shall exclude any shares (x) sold in a public offering or pursuant to Rule 144, or (y) that may be sold pursuant to Rule 144 without volume or manner-of-sale restrictions and without the need for current public information as contemplated by Rule 144(c)(1).

**\"Registration Expenses\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 6.

**\"Rule 144\"** means Rule 144 promulgated by the Commission under the Securities Act, as such rule may be amended from time to time, or any successor rule.

**\"Securities Act\"** means the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder.

**\"Selling Expenses\"** means all underwriting discounts, selling commissions, and stock transfer taxes applicable to the sale of Registrable Securities.

**[Section 2. Registration Rights]{.underline}**

**2.1 Demand Registration Rights.**

**(a) Request for Registration.** At any time after November 3, 2024 (three (3) years from the date of this Agreement), Holders of at least thirty percent (30%) of the then-outstanding Registrable Securities (the **\"Initiating Holders\"**) may request in writing that the Company file a registration statement on Form S-1 (or, if available, Form S-3) covering the resale of all or a portion of the Registrable Securities held by such Initiating Holders (a **\"Demand Registration\"**). Upon receipt of such request, the Company shall promptly (but in no event later than ten (10) days after receipt) give written notice of such request to all other Holders of Registrable Securities and, subject to the limitations set forth in this Section 2.1, shall use commercially reasonable efforts to effect such registration as soon as practicable.

**(b) Number of Demand Registrations.** The Company shall not be obligated to effect more than two (2) Demand Registrations in the aggregate.

**(c) Underwriting.** If the Initiating Holders intend for the Demand Registration to be an underwritten offering, the right of any Holder to include Registrable Securities in such registration shall be conditioned upon such Holder\'s participation in such underwriting. All Holders proposing to distribute their Registrable Securities through such underwriting shall enter into an underwriting agreement in customary form with the managing underwriter(s) selected for such underwriting by the Initiating Holders (which managing underwriter(s) shall be reasonably acceptable to the Company). If the managing underwriter(s) advise(s) the Initiating Holders in writing that the number of Registrable Securities requested to be included in such offering exceeds the number which can be sold in an orderly manner in such offering within a price range acceptable to the Initiating Holders, the Company shall include in such registration:

> \(i\) **first**, the Registrable Securities requested to be included by the Initiating Holders and all other Holders who have requested to participate, allocated pro rata among such Holders based on the number of Registrable Securities requested to be included by each; and
>
> \(ii\) **second**, any securities the Company proposes to register for its own account.

**(d) Deferral Right.** Notwithstanding the foregoing, if the Board of Directors of the Company determines in good faith that the filing of a registration statement would be materially detrimental to the Company and its stockholders because such filing would (i) materially interfere with a significant acquisition, corporate reorganization, or other similar transaction involving the Company, (ii) require premature disclosure of material information that the Company has a bona fide business purpose for preserving as confidential, or (iii) render the Company unable to comply with requirements under the Securities Act or Exchange Act, the Company shall have the right to defer the filing of such registration statement for a period of not more than one hundred twenty (120) days after receipt of the request of the Initiating Holders (a **\"Deferral Period\"**); *provided*, that the Company may not exercise the Deferral Right more than once in any twelve (12)-month period; and *provided, further*, that the Company shall not register any securities for its own account or that of any other stockholder during such Deferral Period (other than on Form S-4 or Form S-8 or any successor forms).

**(e) Effectiveness.** A Demand Registration shall not be deemed to have been effected unless (i) the registration statement has become effective under the Securities Act, (ii) if the registration statement is subject to a stop order, injunction, or similar order of the Commission, such stop order, injunction, or order has been removed, rescinded, or otherwise terminated, and (iii) the registration statement has remained effective for a period of at least one hundred eighty (180) days (or such shorter period in which all Registrable Securities included therein have been sold).

**2.2 Piggyback Registration Rights.**

**(a) Notice.** If at any time the Company proposes to file a registration statement under the Securities Act with respect to an offering of any class of equity securities of the Company (other than a registration statement on Form S-4 or Form S-8 or any successor forms thereto, or a registration statement filed in connection with an exchange offer or an offering of securities solely to existing stockholders of the Company), the Company shall give written notice of such proposed registration to each Holder at least twenty (20) days prior to the anticipated filing date.

**(b) Inclusion.** Each Holder shall have the right, by delivering written notice to the Company within fifteen (15) days after receipt of the Company\'s notice, to request that the Company include in such registration all or a portion of such Holder\'s Registrable Securities (a **\"Piggyback Registration\"**).

**(c) Underwriter Cutback.** If a Piggyback Registration is an underwritten offering and the managing underwriter(s) advise(s) the Company in writing that the total amount of securities requested to be included in such offering exceeds the amount that can be sold in an orderly manner within a price range acceptable to the Company, the Company shall include in such registration:

> \(i\) **first**, all securities the Company proposes to register for its own account;
>
> \(ii\) **second**, the Registrable Securities requested to be included by the Holders, allocated pro rata among such Holders based on the number of Registrable Securities each has requested to be included; and
>
> \(iii\) **third**, any other securities requested to be included in such registration.

**(d) Withdrawal.** Any Holder may withdraw its Registrable Securities from a Piggyback Registration at any time prior to the effective date of the applicable registration statement by providing written notice to the Company. The Company may also withdraw the proposed registration at any time prior to effectiveness, in which case the Piggyback Registration rights shall be deemed not to have been exercised.

**2.3 Form S-3 Registration Rights.**

**(a) S-3 Eligibility.** After the Company becomes eligible to file a registration statement on Form S-3 (or any successor form), any Holder or Holders of Registrable Securities may request in writing that the Company file a registration statement on Form S-3 covering the resale of Registrable Securities; *provided*, that the aggregate offering price of the Registrable Securities to be registered in any single Form S-3 registration shall be at least Five Million Dollars (\$5,000,000) (net of Selling Expenses).

**(b) Unlimited Registrations.** There shall be no limit on the number of Form S-3 registrations that may be requested by the Holders under this Section 2.3.

**(c) Deferral.** The Company\'s Deferral Right set forth in Section 2.1(d) shall apply to any Form S-3 registration request made pursuant to this Section 2.3, *mutatis mutandis*.

**(d) Effectiveness.** The Company shall use its commercially reasonable efforts to cause each Form S-3 registration statement to become effective as soon as practicable after the filing thereof and to remain effective until the earlier of (i) the date on which all Registrable Securities covered by such registration statement have been sold and (ii) the date that is ninety (90) days after the effective date of such registration statement.

**[Section 3. Registration Procedures]{.underline}**

Whenever the Company is required to effect the registration of Registrable Securities under this Agreement, the Company shall, as expeditiously as reasonably practicable:

**(a)** Prepare and file with the Commission a registration statement on the appropriate form with respect to such Registrable Securities and use commercially reasonable efforts to cause such registration statement to become effective as soon as practicable after the filing thereof.

**(b)** Prepare and file with the Commission such amendments and supplements to such registration statement and the prospectus included therein as may be necessary to keep the registration statement effective for the applicable period set forth in this Agreement.

**(c)** Furnish to each selling Holder a reasonable number of copies of such registration statement, each amendment and supplement thereto, each prospectus included therein (including each preliminary prospectus), and such other documents as such Holder may reasonably request in order to facilitate the disposition of the Registrable Securities owned by such Holder.

**(d)** Use commercially reasonable efforts to register or qualify the Registrable Securities under such state securities or \"blue sky\" laws of such jurisdictions as any selling Holder reasonably requests and to keep such registrations and qualifications effective for the applicable period; *provided*, that the Company shall not be required to qualify to do business in any jurisdiction where it is not then so qualified or to take any action that would subject it to taxation or general service of process in any jurisdiction where it is not then so subject.

**(e)** Notify each selling Holder promptly upon discovery that, or upon the happening of any event as a result of which, the registration statement or any prospectus included therein contains an untrue statement of a material fact or omits to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, and promptly prepare and furnish a supplement or amendment to correct such untrue statement or omission.

**(f)** Cooperate with the selling Holders and the managing underwriter(s), if any, in connection with any due diligence investigation reasonably requested in connection with the offering.

**(g)** Use commercially reasonable efforts to cause the Registrable Securities to be listed on any national securities exchange on which similar securities of the Company are then listed or, if no such listing then exists, on a nationally recognized securities exchange.

**(h)** Provide a transfer agent and registrar for all Registrable Securities covered by such registration statement. The Company\'s current transfer agent is Pinnacle Trust Company, located in Wilmington, Delaware.

**(i)** Enter into and perform its obligations under customary agreements (including, in the case of an underwritten offering, an underwriting agreement in customary form) and take all other actions reasonably necessary to expedite or facilitate the disposition of such Registrable Securities.

**(j)** Make available senior management of the Company for participation in customary \"road show\" presentations in connection with any underwritten offering, at such times and places as the managing underwriter(s) may reasonably request.

**[Section 4. Lock-Up Agreement]{.underline}**

**(a) Holder Lock-Up.** Each Holder of Registrable Securities agrees that, if requested by the managing underwriter(s) in connection with any underwritten public offering by the Company, such Holder shall not, without the prior written consent of such managing underwriter(s), during the period commencing on the date of the final prospectus relating to such offering and ending on the date that is one hundred eighty (180) days after the date of such final prospectus (or such shorter period as the managing underwriter(s) may specify), (i) offer, pledge, sell, contract to sell, sell any option or contract to purchase, purchase any option or contract to sell, grant any option, right, or warrant to purchase, lend, or otherwise transfer or dispose of, directly or indirectly, any shares of Common Stock or any securities convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for Common Stock, or (ii) enter into any swap or other arrangement that transfers to another, in whole or in part, any of the economic consequences of ownership of Common Stock, regardless of whether any transaction described in clause (i) or (ii) above is to be settled by delivery of Common Stock or such other securities, in cash, or otherwise.

**(b) Company Lock-Up.** The Company agrees that, during any lock-up period applicable to the Holders under this Section 4, the Company shall not, without the prior written consent of the managing underwriter(s), effect any public sale or distribution of its equity securities (other than on Form S-4 or Form S-8 or any successor forms thereto).

**(c) Exceptions.** The restrictions in Section 4(a) shall not apply to (i) securities included in the applicable registration or (ii) transfers to affiliates or family members of such Holder who agree in writing to be bound by the same restrictions for the remainder of the applicable lock-up period.

**[Section 5. Market Stand-Off]{.underline}**

Each Holder agrees that, in connection with any underwritten public offering by the Company, such Holder shall enter into a customary market stand-off agreement with the managing underwriter(s) on terms no less restrictive than this Section 5, for a period of one hundred eighty (180) days following the effective date of such registration statement, restricting such Holder from selling, making any short sale of, loaning, granting any option for the purchase of, or otherwise disposing of, any Registrable Securities (other than those included in such registration) without the prior written consent of the Company and the managing underwriter(s).

The Company shall use commercially reasonable efforts to require all executive officers, directors, and holders of more than five percent (5%) of the Company\'s voting securities to enter into market stand-off agreements containing substantially similar restrictions for the same period. This Section 5 shall apply only in the context of an underwritten public offering.

**[Section 6. Registration Expenses]{.underline}**

**\"Registration Expenses\"** shall mean all expenses incurred by the Company in connection with effecting any registration or qualification under this Agreement, including without limitation: (i) all registration and filing fees; (ii) fees and expenses of compliance with securities or \"blue sky\" laws; (iii) printing and duplication expenses; (iv) the Company\'s internal expenses (including salaries and expenses of its officers and employees performing legal or accounting duties); (v) fees and disbursements of counsel for the Company; (vi) fees and disbursements of the Company\'s independent registered public accounting firm; (vii) fees and expenses of any special experts or consultants retained by the Company in connection with such registration; (viii) reasonable fees and disbursements of one counsel selected by the Holders of a majority of the Registrable Securities being registered, in an amount not to exceed Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$50,000) per registration; and (ix) fees and expenses associated with any FINRA filing.

The Company shall bear all Registration Expenses in connection with any registration effected pursuant to Sections 2.1, 2.2, or 2.3 of this Agreement.

Selling Expenses (including underwriting discounts, selling commissions, and stock transfer taxes) shall be borne by each selling Holder pro rata based on the number of Registrable Securities sold by such Holder in the applicable offering.

**[Section 7. Indemnification]{.underline}**

**7.1 Company Indemnification.**

The Company shall indemnify and hold harmless each selling Holder, its officers, directors, partners, members, managers, employees, and agents, and each Person who controls such Holder within the meaning of Section 15 of the Securities Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and expenses of investigation) arising out of or based upon (i) any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in any registration statement, any prospectus included therein, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or (ii) any omission or alleged omission to state therein a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading; *provided*, *however*, that the Company shall not be liable for any such losses, claims, damages, liabilities, or expenses to the extent that they arise out of or are based upon any untrue statement or omission made in reliance upon and in conformity with information furnished in writing by or on behalf of such Holder expressly for use therein.

**7.2 Holder Indemnification.**

Each selling Holder shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless the Company, its directors, its officers, and each Person who controls the Company within the meaning of Section 15 of the Securities Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated in, the registration statement, any prospectus included therein, or any amendment or supplement thereto, but only to the extent that such untrue statement or omission was made in reliance upon and in conformity with information furnished in writing by such Holder expressly for inclusion therein.

The liability of each selling Holder under this Section 7.2 shall in no event exceed the amount of net proceeds received by such Holder from the sale of Registrable Securities pursuant to such registration.

**7.3 Contribution.** If the indemnification provided for in Sections 7.1 and 7.2 is unavailable to or insufficient to hold harmless an indemnified party in respect of any losses, claims, damages, liabilities, or expenses for which such indemnified party would otherwise be entitled to indemnification, then the indemnifying party, in lieu of indemnifying such indemnified party, shall contribute to the amount paid or payable by such indemnified party as a result of such losses, claims, damages, liabilities, or expenses in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect the relative fault of the Company on the one hand and the selling Holders on the other hand in connection with the statements or omissions giving rise to such losses, as well as any other relevant equitable considerations. No selling Holder shall be required to contribute any amount in excess of the net proceeds received by such Holder from the sale of Registrable Securities pursuant to such registration.

**[Section 8. Information Rights and Reporting]{.underline}**

The Company shall use commercially reasonable efforts to comply with the reporting requirements of the Exchange Act (to the extent applicable) and to furnish to each Holder, promptly upon request, such information as may be necessary to enable such Holder to sell Registrable Securities pursuant to Rule 144.

If the Company is not subject to the reporting requirements of the Exchange Act, the Company shall, upon the request of any Holder, make available \"current public information\" as defined in Rule 144(c)(2) under the Securities Act for so long as such information is necessary to permit sales of Registrable Securities under Rule 144.

**[Section 9. Termination]{.underline}**

The registration rights granted under this Agreement shall terminate with respect to any particular Holder upon the earliest to occur of:

> **(a)** the date on which such Holder can sell all of its Registrable Securities in a single three (3)-month period pursuant to Rule 144 under the Securities Act without volume or manner-of-sale restrictions and without the requirement for the Company to be in compliance with the current public information requirement of Rule 144(c)(1);
>
> **(b)** the fifth (5th) anniversary of the closing of the Company\'s IPO; or
>
> **(c)** a Deemed Liquidation Event.

This Agreement shall terminate in its entirety upon the earliest to occur of (i) the termination of registration rights as to all Holders pursuant to clauses (a), (b), or (c) above, or (ii) the written consent of the Company and Holders of at least a majority of the then-outstanding Registrable Securities. Any termination of this Agreement pursuant to this Section 9 shall be without prejudice to (x) the rights and obligations of the parties under Section 7 (Indemnification), which shall survive any such termination, and (y) any liability for breach of this Agreement prior to such termination.

**[Section 10. Assignment and Transfer]{.underline}**

The rights of any Holder under this Agreement may be assigned or transferred to any transferee or assignee of Registrable Securities; *provided*, that (a) such transferee or assignee acquires at least 500,000 shares of Registrable Securities (as adjusted for stock splits, stock dividends, recapitalizations, and the like), (b) such transfer is effected in accordance with applicable securities laws, and (c) such transferee or assignee executes a written joinder agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A, agreeing to be bound by all terms and conditions of this Agreement.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Holder may assign its rights hereunder to an Affiliate or a partner, member, or stockholder of such Holder without restriction as to the minimum share threshold, provided such assignee agrees in writing to be bound by this Agreement.

**[Section 11. Miscellaneous]{.underline}**

**11.1 Governing Law.**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to principles of conflicts of law that would cause the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

**11.2 Entire Agreement; Amendment.**

This Agreement, together with the Purchase Agreement and the other Transaction Documents (as defined in the Purchase Agreement), constitutes the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, including the Prior Agreement, with respect to the subject matter hereof.

This Agreement may be amended, modified, or supplemented only by a written instrument signed by (a) the Company and (b) Holders of at least a majority of the then-outstanding Registrable Securities.

**11.3 Notices.** All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by (i) personal delivery, (ii) nationally recognized overnight courier, (iii) certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, or (iv) email (with confirmation of receipt), to the addresses set forth on Schedule A (for Holders) or to the Company at:

> **Terravox Systems, Inc.** 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300 Austin, TX 78723
>
> Attention: Chief Executive Officer
>
> with a copy (which shall not constitute notice) to:
>
> **Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP** 100 Congress Avenue, Suite 2400 Austin, TX 78701
>
> Attention: Nathaniel Rourke

Any notice delivered personally or by overnight courier shall be deemed received upon delivery. Any notice sent by certified or registered mail shall be deemed received three (3) business days after mailing. Any notice sent by email shall be deemed received upon confirmation of receipt. Any party may change its address for notice purposes by giving written notice of such change to the other parties in accordance with this Section 11.3.

**11.4 Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect, and the invalid or unenforceable provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid and enforceable.

**11.5 Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same agreement. Delivery of an executed signature page by electronic transmission (including by portable document format (.pdf) or similar means) shall be effective as delivery of a manually executed counterpart.

**11.6 Waiver.** No waiver of any breach or default hereunder shall be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach or default of the same or similar nature. No waiver shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the party against whom enforcement of such waiver is sought.

**11.7 Remedies.** Each party acknowledges that a breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy and that, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, the non-breaching party shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including specific performance and injunctive relief, without the need to post a bond or other security.

**11.8 Delays or Omissions.**

No delay or omission to exercise any right, power, or remedy accruing to any party under this Agreement shall impair any such right, power, or remedy or shall be construed as a waiver thereof. All remedies under this Agreement shall be cumulative and shall not be exclusive of any other remedy available to any party at law, in equity, or otherwise.

**11.9 Aggregation of Shares.**

All shares of Registrable Securities held or acquired by affiliated entities or persons shall be aggregated together for the purpose of determining the availability of any rights under this Agreement, including the threshold requirements set forth in Sections 2.1(a) and 10.

text: I now have the Voting Agreement and RRA. Critical findings: (1) the RRA terminates on a "Deemed Liquidation Event" but that term is **undefined** in the RRA — a definitional gap; (2) the Voting Agreement's "Sale of the Company" matches the Stockholders' Agreement; (3) Sandra Okafor is Northbridge's designee (Series B Director) — relevant to the Special Committee independence concern. Now let me read the Board Minutes, which are essential for Areas 7, 8, and 9.

**January 2023 through September 2024**

These minutes are compiled from the official records of Terravox Systems, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"Company\"), maintained at its principal offices at 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723. The Company is incorporated in the State of Delaware under File No. DE-5721438. The Company\'s Secretary, Linda Yeung, who also serves as the Company\'s Chief Financial Officer, served as recording secretary for all meetings of the Board of Directors during the period covered by this compilation unless otherwise noted herein.

These minutes should be read in conjunction with the Company\'s Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation dated November 3, 2021 (the \"Restated Certificate\"), the Amended and Restated Bylaws of the Company, last amended November 3, 2021 (the \"Bylaws\"), and the governance agreements executed as of November 3, 2021, including the Investors\' Rights Agreement, the Voting Agreement, the Right of First Refusal and Co-Sale Agreement, and the Stockholders\' Agreement (collectively, the \"Governance Agreements\").

**[Meeting 1: Minutes of Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors --- January 18, 2023]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A regular meeting of the Board of Directors (the \"Board\") of Terravox Systems, Inc. (the \"Company\") was held on **January 18, 2023**, commencing at **10:00 a.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference.

**Directors Present:**

> • Marcus Ellery, Chairman
>
> • Priya Chandrasekaran
>
> • David Kleinhans
>
> • Sandra Okafor
>
> • Julian Trask
>
> • Rebecca Lau
>
> • Thomas Garrett

All seven (7) directors then in office were present, constituting a quorum pursuant to Article III of the Amended and Restated Bylaws (majority of directors then in office = 4).

**Also Present:**

> • Linda Yeung, Chief Financial Officer and recording secretary
>
> • Nathaniel Rourke, Esq., Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, outside counsel

**Board Observer:** A representative of Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP was present in an observer capacity pursuant to the Voting Agreement dated November 3, 2021.

**[Call to Order and Preliminary Matters]{.underline}**

Chairman Ellery called the meeting to order at 10:00 a.m. Central Time. The Chairman noted that proper notice of the meeting had been given to all directors in accordance with the Bylaws. The Secretary confirmed the presence of a quorum. The minutes of the prior regular meeting of the Board held on October 19, 2022, were presented for review. A motion to approve the minutes of the October 2022 meeting was made by Mr. Kleinhans and seconded by Ms. Lau. The minutes were unanimously approved.

**[FY2023 Operating Budget]{.underline}**

Ms. Yeung, in her capacity as Chief Financial Officer, presented the proposed annual operating budget for fiscal year 2023 (\"FY2023\"). Key highlights of the proposed budget included the following: projected revenue of \$120,000,000 to \$130,000,000; projected Adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 24% to 26%; a capital expenditure budget of \$8,500,000; and a headcount plan targeting approximately 425 employees by fiscal year-end. Ms. Yeung reviewed the assumptions underlying the revenue projections, including anticipated growth in the Company\'s recurring sensor-as-a-service revenue streams and the expected contribution from the Permian Basin opportunity described below.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the FY2023 annual operating budget of the Company, in the form presented to the Board at this meeting, with such modifications as the CEO and CFO may deem appropriate in the ordinary course of business.

Motion by Mr. Garrett, seconded by Ms. Okafor. The resolution was unanimously approved (7-0).

**[Permian Basin Energy Co. Master Services Agreement]{.underline}**

CEO Ellery reported to the Board on a significant commercial opportunity with Permian Basin Energy Co. (\"Permian Basin\") for the deployment of the Company\'s industrial IoT sensor platform across Permian Basin\'s oil and gas field operations in West Texas and southeastern New Mexico. Mr. Ellery described the opportunity as encompassing the installation of approximately 12,000 sensor nodes, along with the Company\'s predictive analytics software suite and ongoing data management services. The estimated contract value was up to \$15,000,000 in aggregate consideration over a three-year term. Mr. Ellery noted that the Company\'s business development team had been in discussions with Permian Basin\'s operations leadership for approximately four months and that commercial terms were substantially negotiated.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the Chief Executive Officer to negotiate and execute a master services agreement with Permian Basin Energy Co. for the deployment of the Company\'s industrial IoT sensor platform, for aggregate consideration of up to \$15,000,000, on terms substantially consistent with those presented to the Board at this meeting.

Motion by Ms. Chandrasekaran, seconded by Mr. Trask. The resolution was unanimously approved (7-0).

**[Discussion of Strategic Alternatives]{.underline}**

Chairman Ellery raised the topic of potential strategic alternatives for the Company, including a possible sale, merger, or other liquidity event. Mr. Ellery observed that the industrial IoT sector had seen several significant transactions in recent quarters and that the Company\'s growth trajectory and competitive position might make the current environment favorable for exploring options to maximize stockholder value.

A discussion among the directors followed. Several directors expressed interest in understanding what options might be available to the Company. Mr. Kleinhans noted that Ridgeline Ventures, as a significant stockholder, was generally supportive of evaluating strategic alternatives, particularly given the maturity of its fund. Mr. Trask noted that Stonemill Capital Partners would want to be kept closely informed of any process undertaken by the Company and emphasized the importance of transparency with all major stockholders. Ms. Lau observed that it would be prudent to engage a financial advisor to provide market perspective and manage any process in a structured manner.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the Chief Executive Officer and the Chief Financial Officer to engage Meridian Partners LLC as financial advisor to explore strategic alternatives for the Company, including a potential sale, merger, recapitalization, or other transaction that may maximize value for the Company\'s stockholders, on terms to be negotiated and approved by the Chief Executive Officer.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Mr. Kleinhans. The resolution was unanimously approved (7-0).

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

No other business was raised for discussion. The next regular meeting of the Board was scheduled for April 2023. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 12:15 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**[Meeting 2: Minutes of Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors --- April 12, 2023]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A regular meeting of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. was held on **April 12, 2023**, commencing at **10:00 a.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference.

**Directors Present:**

> • Marcus Ellery, Chairman
>
> • Priya Chandrasekaran
>
> • David Kleinhans
>
> • Sandra Okafor
>
> • Julian Trask
>
> • Rebecca Lau
>
> • Thomas Garrett

All seven (7) directors then in office were present, constituting a quorum pursuant to Article III of the Bylaws.

**Also Present:**

> • Linda Yeung, Chief Financial Officer and recording secretary
>
> • Nathaniel Rourke, Esq., Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, outside counsel
>
> • Representatives of Meridian Partners LLC, financial advisor

**Board Observer:** A representative of Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP was present in an observer capacity.

**[Approval of Prior Minutes]{.underline}**

The minutes of the regular meeting of the Board held on January 18, 2023, were presented for review. A motion to approve was made by Ms. Okafor and seconded by Ms. Chandrasekaran. The minutes were unanimously approved.

**[Meridian Partners Presentation --- Strategic Alternatives]{.underline}**

Representatives of Meridian Partners LLC presented an update on the strategic alternatives process initiated following the Board\'s authorization at its January 18, 2023 meeting. Meridian reported that it had contacted a targeted group of potential acquirers, including both strategic buyers and private equity sponsors, and had received preliminary indications of interest from three (3) parties (identified by code names: \"Alpha,\" \"Beta,\" and \"Gamma\"). Meridian provided a summary of each indication of interest, including preliminary valuation ranges and proposed transaction structures. \"Alpha\" had indicated an enterprise value range of \$350,000,000 to \$400,000,000 in an all-cash transaction. \"Beta\" had indicated an enterprise value range of \$450,000,000 to \$525,000,000 in a leveraged buyout structure. \"Gamma\" had indicated an enterprise value range of \$380,000,000 to \$420,000,000 in a stock-for-stock strategic merger.

Discussion among the Board followed regarding the merits and risks of each potential acquirer, including certainty of closing, integration considerations, and implied valuation multiples. Mr. Trask asked detailed questions about each potential acquirer\'s financial capacity and strategic rationale, and emphasized that any process must be designed to maximize value for all stockholders, including holders of the Company\'s Series C Preferred Stock. Meridian recommended that the Board consider forming a special committee of independent directors to evaluate the strategic alternatives and oversee any potential transaction process.

**[Formation of Special Committee]{.underline}**

Outside counsel Rourke addressed the Board regarding the formation and mandate of a special committee to evaluate strategic alternatives. Mr. Rourke advised that it was customary and prudent in connection with a potential sale or change-of-control transaction for the Board to delegate oversight of the process to a committee of independent and disinterested directors, with authority to engage independent advisors and to make recommendations to the full Board.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the formation of a Special Committee of the Board (the \"Special Committee\") consisting of Rebecca Lau (Chair), Thomas Garrett, and Sandra Okafor, with authority to (a) evaluate strategic alternatives for the Company, including a potential sale, merger, or other change-of-control transaction, (b) engage financial advisors, legal counsel, and other advisors as the Special Committee deems appropriate, and (c) make recommendations to the full Board regarding any proposed transaction.

Ms. Okafor\'s service on the Special Committee was noted. Ms. Okafor is a director designated by Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP pursuant to Section 1.2(c) of the Voting Agreement dated November 3, 2021.

Motion by Mr. Ellery, seconded by Mr. Kleinhans. The resolution was approved (7-0).

**[Stock Option Grant --- Raj Malhotra]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, in her capacity as Chair of the Compensation Committee, presented a recommendation to grant stock options to Raj Malhotra, the Company\'s newly hired Vice President of Engineering. The Compensation Committee had reviewed comparable market data and recommended a grant of 350,000 stock options under the Company\'s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan (the \"Plan\"), which was adopted on November 3, 2021. The exercise price was proposed at \$14.30 per share, which was determined to be the fair market value of the Company\'s Common Stock as of the grant date based on the most recent independent 409A valuation report. The options would vest over four (4) years, with a one (1) year cliff (25% vesting on the first anniversary of the vesting commencement date), and monthly thereafter over the remaining 36 months, subject to Mr. Malhotra\'s continued service with the Company.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the grant of 350,000 stock options to Raj Malhotra under the Company\'s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan at an exercise price of \$14.30 per share, vesting over four (4) years with a one (1) year cliff, on terms consistent with the Company\'s standard form of stock option agreement approved for use under the Plan.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Mr. Garrett. The resolution was unanimously approved (7-0).

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

No other business was raised for discussion. The next regular meeting of the Board was scheduled for July 2023. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 1:30 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**[Meeting 3: Minutes of Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors --- July 19, 2023]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A regular meeting of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. was held on **July 19, 2023**, commencing at **10:00 a.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference.

**Directors Present:**

> • Marcus Ellery, Chairman
>
> • Priya Chandrasekaran
>
> • David Kleinhans
>
> • Sandra Okafor
>
> • Julian Trask
>
> • Rebecca Lau
>
> • Thomas Garrett

All seven (7) directors then in office were present, constituting a quorum pursuant to Article III of the Bylaws.

**Also Present:**

> • Linda Yeung, Chief Financial Officer and recording secretary
>
> • Nathaniel Rourke, Esq., Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, outside counsel

**Board Observer:** A representative of Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP was present in an observer capacity.

**[Approval of Prior Minutes]{.underline}**

The minutes of the regular meeting of the Board held on April 12, 2023, were presented for review. A motion to approve was made by Mr. Trask and seconded by Mr. Garrett. The minutes were unanimously approved.

**[Quarterly Financial Review --- Q2 2023]{.underline}**

Ms. Yeung presented the Company\'s financial results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2023 (\"Q2 2023\"). Revenue for Q2 2023 was \$31,200,000, reflecting an annualized run rate of approximately \$124,800,000. Gross margin was 68%. Operating expenses were on budget across all departments. Ms. Yeung noted that the Company was tracking toward the upper end of FY2023 revenue guidance of \$120,000,000 to \$130,000,000, driven by strong performance in the Permian Basin Energy Co. engagement and continued growth in the Company\'s recurring revenue base. The Company\'s cash and cash equivalents were approximately \$24,000,000 as of the end of Q2 2023. Headcount stood at 398 employees.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby accepts the Q2 2023 financial report as presented by the Chief Financial Officer.

Motion by Mr. Garrett, seconded by Mr. Kleinhans. The resolution was unanimously approved.

**[Special Committee Update --- Strategic Alternatives]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, Chair of the Special Committee, reported on the status of the strategic alternatives process. Meridian Partners had conducted preliminary diligence sessions with all three potential acquirers previously identified. Ms. Lau reported that two of the three parties --- code names \"Alpha\" and \"Gamma\" --- had either withdrawn from the process or reduced their indications of interest to valuation levels that the Special Committee deemed inadequate. Discussions with the remaining interested party (code name \"Beta\") were continuing, and Beta had expressed a willingness to proceed with confirmatory due diligence. The Special Committee recommended that the Board authorize the Company to enter into an exclusivity period with \"Beta\" for a period of 45 days to allow for focused diligence and negotiation of preliminary terms.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the Company, acting through the Chief Executive Officer, to enter into an exclusivity agreement with the remaining interested party (code name \"Beta\") for a period of up to forty-five (45) days, on terms approved by the Special Committee.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Ms. Okafor. The resolution was approved 6-1. Mr. Trask voted against the resolution, stating that he believed the process had been insufficiently broad and that Stonemill Capital Partners would prefer a wider auction involving additional potential acquirers before the Company committed to an exclusivity arrangement with a single party.

**[Amendment to 2021 Equity Incentive Plan --- Share Reserve Increase]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, in her capacity as Chair of the Compensation Committee, presented a proposal to amend the Company\'s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan (the \"Plan\") to increase the share reserve available for issuance under the Plan. Ms. Lau explained that the existing share reserve of 8,000,000 shares was substantially allocated, with approximately 7,420,000 shares subject to outstanding awards or previously exercised, leaving fewer than 580,000 shares available for future grants. Ms. Lau stated that additional shares were needed to attract and retain key engineering and management talent as the Company continued to scale its operations and pursue growth opportunities, including the Permian Basin engagement and the ongoing strategic alternatives process.

The Compensation Committee recommended increasing the share reserve by 1,500,000 shares, from the current 8,000,000 shares to 9,500,000 shares. The Board discussed the dilutive impact of the proposed increase. Ms. Yeung presented an analysis demonstrating that the proposed increase would raise the fully diluted option pool from approximately 16.7% to approximately 20.1% of the Company\'s fully diluted capitalization, which the Compensation Committee believed to be within the range of market benchmarks for companies at a comparable stage and size. Outside counsel Rourke noted that the amendment required Board approval.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the amendment to the Company\'s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan to increase the share reserve by 1,500,000 shares, from 8,000,000 shares to 9,500,000 shares, effective as of the date hereof, and the officers of the Company are hereby authorized to take all actions necessary to effectuate such amendment.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Ms. Chandrasekaran. The resolution was approved (7-0).

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

No other business was raised for discussion. The next regular meeting of the Board was scheduled for October 2023. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 12:45 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**[Meeting 4: Minutes of Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors --- October 11, 2023]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A regular meeting of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. was held on **October 11, 2023**, commencing at **10:00 a.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference.

**Directors Present:**
